items.title: Canada krijgt derde rode kaart in vier oefenduels: 'We zijn geen smerig team'
items.summary: Canada kreeg zaterdag tegen IJsland (2-2) voor de derde keer in vier oefenwedstrijden een rode kaart. Villarreal-middenvelder Tajon Buchanan werd in de slotfase van het veld gestuurd na een vermeende elleboogstoot. Bondscoach Jesse Marsch verdedigde zijn ploeg na afloop.

items.title: Goal-shy Leicester rooted to bottom of WSL but manager and fans not giving up
items.summary: Relegation playoff against a WSL2 side beckons if Rick Passmoor’s team cannot end seven-game losing streak The sight of two unwaveringly optimistic young girls staunchly waving their “Foxes never quit” flags proudly in the air – despite the swirling rain at the King Power Stadium – summed up the never-say-die attitude required for a relegation battle that Leicester are going to need now more than ever, after their chances of staying up decreased significantly with Sunday’s defeat. Even before losing against Brighton, Leicester’s hopes had suffered a big blow with the sight of Oona Siren hitting a superb, looping volley into the net to earn 11th-placed West Ham a valuable point in a lunchtime kick-off. The 1-1 draw at home against London City Lionesses edged West Ham further away from the bottom side Leicester, who would go on to be deservedly beaten 1-0 by Brighton and find themselves four points adrift with four games remaining. Continue reading...

items.title: Meerdink, Hofs, piepjonge Memphis en WK-opluchting: dit waren de duels met Ecuador
items.summary: Oranje en Ecuador staan dinsdag pas voor de vierde keer tegenover elkaar in een officiële interland. Nederland verloor nog nooit van de Zuid-Amerikanen en doelpunten bleken spaarzaam in de eerdere ontmoetingen.

items.title: 'Bayern krijgt goed keepersnieuws, Courtois zeer onzeker voor CL-clash'
items.summary: Manuel Neuer is fit genoeg om in actie te komen in de kwartfinale van de Champions League tegen Real Madrid. Aan de andere kant zijn er juist zorgen over Thibaut Courtois.

items.title: USMNT routed by Belgium in blow to WCup prep
items.summary: The USMNT suffered a setback in its preparations for the World Cup as Belgium ran rampant in the second half to emerge with a 5-2 win at Atlanta's Mercedes-Benz Stadium on Saturday.

items.title: Pulisic, U.S. 'shocked' by Belgium jersey clash
items.summary: Christian Pulisic was among several U.S. players who said that the similarly colored jerseys worn by Belgium and the USMNT made it more difficult to pick out teammates.

items.title: Denver sets NWSL attendance record at Mile High
items.summary: Denver Summit set the NWSL attendance record in their inaugural home game, bringing in a crowd of 63,004 against the Washington Spirit.

items.title: Bompastor: Chelsea not 'good enough' in Villa win
items.summary: Chelsea manager Sonia Bompastor has lamented her side's defensive showing in their dramatic 4-3 win over Aston Villa in the Women's Super League (WSL).

items.title: Feyenoord krijgt tik in titelstrijd, vreemde rode kaart PEC Zwolle
items.summary: In dit blog volgen we alle ontwikkelingen in het vrouwenvoetbal. We besteden onder meer aandacht aan de Eurojackpot Vrouwen Eredivisie, de Europese toernooien en het Nederlands elftal.

items.title: Tuchel’s England? Maybe they are just not as good as we would like them to be | Barney Ronay
items.summary: The Three Lions have not beaten a good side under their coach and no A-list players have emerged since the last World Cup Maybe we’re just not that into us. There are times when trying to rationalise the makeup, reach and ultimate capacities of the England football team can feel a bit like living inside the frantically hyper-formalised New York dating scene of the 1990s. Here we go again. Picking over the details. Hung up on what-ifs. Arguing about The Rules of the Game. Don’t be too available. Never text first. Do wear a wizard hat. Learn magic tricks. And be rude to people. Also, be endlessly mysterious. No, more mysterious than that. Seriously, where do you get off not having enough mystery? Continue reading...

items.title: Veranderingen bij Afrika Cup: meer deelnemende landen in 2027
items.summary: De Afrika Cup wordt uitgebreid. Het toernooi gaat van 24 deelnemers naar 28. In 2027 wordt er voor het eerst met dat aantal landen gespeeld.

items.title: Spurs have addressed their Tudor error - but cannot afford another
items.summary: Chief executive Vinai Venkatesham and sporting director Johan Lange must get the next Tottenham appointment correct or else they could follow Igor Tudor, writes Phil McNulty.

items.title: What might Scotland change for Ivory Coast test?
items.summary: After Saturday's friendly defeat by Japan, what might Scotland tweak for Tuesday's test against Ivory Coast?

items.title: Tudor leaves Tottenham after just seven games
items.summary: Tottenham Hotspur have parted ways with Igor Tudor amid a worrying battle to avoid relegation from the Premier League

items.title: BBC Sport experts choose England World Cup XIs - who would you pick?
items.summary: With one game to go until Thomas Tuchel picks his England World Cup squad, BBC Sport reporters choose their starting XIs for the summer. Who would you pick?

items.title: Igor Tudor leaves Tottenham after 44 days with club mired in relegation trouble
items.summary: Croat failed to win a league match as interim head coach Spurs hope to name Tudor’s successor in coming days Tottenham have parted company with Igor Tudor after seven games and 44 days in a desperate attempt to halt their slide towards relegation from the Premier League. According to the club, the decision was mutually agreed. Tudor took one point from his five league matches to leave Tottenham one point and one place above the bottom three, the final straw coming with last Sunday’s 3-0 home defeat by Nottingham Forest. A previously unthinkable demotion to the Championship would be devastating for prestige and revenues. Continue reading...

items.title: Tudor leaves Spurs after just 44 days in charge
items.summary: Igor Tudor leaves Tottenham Hotspur with immediate effect.

items.title: Caf general secretary resigns amid fallout from Afcon final controversy
items.summary: Véron Mosengo-Omba was target of varied criticism ‘I can retire with peace of mind and without constraint’ Véron Mosengo-Omba, the Confederation of African Football (Caf) general secretary, has resigned after repeated calls for his removal and at a turbulent time for the game on the continent. Mosengo-Omba said he was retiring but his departure comes amid a crisis of confidence in the organisation’s leadership, with a growing fallout over the decision to strip Senegal of the Africa Cup of Nations (Afcon) title and calls for an investigation into alleged corruption at African ­football’s governing body. Continue reading...

items.title: Why are Scotland playing Ivory Coast in Liverpool?
items.summary: Scotland continue their World Cup preparations with a friendly against Ivory Coast on Tuesday, but why is this away game being played at Everton's new stadium in Liverpool?

items.title: 🎥 52 ans et toujours aussi dangereux : un ancien d'Anderlecht fait parler la poudre à Anfield
items.summary: Ce week-end, les légendes de Liverpool ont croisé le fer avec celles de Dortmund. Un certain Jan Koller avait montré qu'il n'avait rien perdu de son sens du but.

items.title: Contre les consignes de Martinez : Toby Alderweireld revient sur son altercation avec Kevin De Bruyne au Qatar
items.summary: Lors de la Coupe du Monde, les Diables Rouges devront se reposer une ambiance plus sereine dans le groupe qu'en 2022. Toby Alderweireld revient sur son moment sous tension avec Kevin De Bruyne.

items.title: 📷 'Interesse groeit voor Onuachu: ook deze knappe deals zijn een optie'
items.summary: Paul Onuachu zet een sterk seizoen neer bij Trabzonspor. En dat wekt enorm veel interesse voor de ex-aanvaller van KRC Genk.

items.title: "Il ne pouvait pas revenir sans" : la soirée dans un restaurant bavarois qui a tout changé pour Kompany
items.summary: Vincent Kompany continue d'impressionner au Bayern Munich, mais sa nomination n'était pas exempte de doutes à l'époque. Dans une interview accordée à Kicker, Uli Hoeneß est longuement revenu sur la manière dont le Belge a finalement été recruté.

items.title: OFFICIEEL Trainersontslag na slechts vijf matchen (maar niet de snelste ooit in de competitie)
items.summary: Tottenham Hotspur beëindigde de samenwerking met trainer Igor Tudor na een bijzonder korte en turbulente periode. Hij hield het amper 44 dagen vol bij de Engelse club.

items.title: La décision est prise : un club de Pro League laisse de côté son transfert le plus cher de tous les temps
items.summary: Hassane Bandé n'a pas convaincu pour son retour à Malines. Il devra se trouver un autre club pour la saison prochaine.

items.title: 'Contractnieuws bij KV Mechelen: beslissing over drie opties in contract genomen'
items.summary: KV Mechelen blinkt de komende weken in de Champions' Play-offs. Ondertussen wordt er ook werk gemaakt van de ploeg voor volgend seizoen.

items.title: La presse américaine étrille les USA après la défaite face aux Diables Rouges
items.summary: Aux États-Unis, les attentes étaient particulièrement élevées avant le duel face à la Belgique. Mais ces ambitions ont pris un sacré coup sur la tête.

items.title: 'Vertrek in de maak? Deze ploeg zet Oosting op shortlist om trainer te worden'
items.summary: Joseph Oosting werd dit seizoen binnengehaald bij Royal Antwerp FC. Al zou het zo maar kunnen dat hij na het seizoen weer weg is.

items.title: Bizarre anekdote over Kompany-deal bij Bayern: "Zonder contract moest hij niet terugkeren"
items.summary: Vincent Kompany blijft indruk maken bij FC Bayern München, maar zijn aanstelling kwam er destijds niet zonder twijfels. In een interview met Kicker blikte Uli Hoeneß uitgebreid terug op hoe de Belg uiteindelijk werd binnengehaald.

items.title: Running on empty? Premier League teams falter under weight of endless schedule | Jonathan Wilson
items.summary: Players are not covering the distances of old – they are not being lazy but adapting to demands of an arduous campaign There is nothing English football admires more than honest endeavour, which is perhaps a consequence of the league’s origins in the industrial cities of the north and Midlands. “He put in a shift.” “She did her job.” “He gave his all.” The language of football is the language of the pit or the factory floor. All top-level players these days are supremely skilled, but still we demand that they be exhausted by the final whistle, legs leaden with effort, hair soaked with sweat. Which was why it seemed to cause such consternation when Alan Shearer mentioned on Match of the Day last Saturday that Chelsea have run less than their opponents in every Premier League game they have played this season. Continue reading...
